perturbation.original_text: 1.5 "FIELD OF USE" means, and is limited to, the utilization of the Licensed Technology by the Licensee for the exclusive purpose of fulfilling Spot FX transactions by transmitting market data and orders. The "Field of Use" shall not include any functions or applications not expressly described in the preceding sentence, but shall include functions or applications developed by Licensor to enhance the performance within the "Field of Use".
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 "FIELD OF USE" means the utilization of the Licensed Technology by the Licensee for Spot FX transactions, as well as any other financial instrument or market the Licensee chooses to trade. The 'Field of Use' includes all functions and applications the Licensee deems necessary for its business operations related to trading.
perturbation.explanation: This change broadens the definition of 'Field of Use' to include any financial instrument or market and all functions the Licensee deems necessary. This contradicts the original text which limited the 'Field of Use' to Spot FX transactions and only those applications developed by the Licensor to enhance performance within that field. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the scope of the license and the Licensee's permitted activities within the license agreement. The contradiction appears and affects the enforcement of Article II, License Grant and Restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE II LICENSE GRANT AND RESTRICTIONS I. Source Code means fully documented human-readable source code form of the Software, including programmer's notes and materials and documentation, sufficient to allow a reasonably skilled programmer to understand the design, logic, structure, functionality, operation and features and to use, operate, maintain, modify, support and diagnose errors. Object Code means Software in machine-readable form that is substantially or entirely in binary form or otherwise directly executable by a computer after processing or linking. i. The Licensee has independently evaluated the system, and has concluded that use of the system confers a significant benefit to the Licensee. Accordingly the Licensee hereby assumes all liabilities and risks associated with the use of the system and equipment, except for direct damages arising from gross negligence or willful misconduct of the Licensor. iii. Licensor hereby, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and provided that Licensee makes payments to Licensor as required under this Agreement, grants to Licensee a non-exclusive license to utilize Licensed Technology solely in the Field of Use and subject to the additional restrictions set forth below and otherwise in this License Agreement. iv. The Licensee's rights to access and use the Licensed Technology is limited to Licensor's operating hours, on days that the foreign exchange markets are open for trading ("FX") ("Market Day"). At the end of the Licensor's operating hours or each market day the system will automatically transmit cancellation messages for each open ticket that is processed on the system or on any of the Pools of Liquidity at that time. There is no guarantee that these cancellations of the open tickets, nor that such tickets will not result in executions outside of the Licensor's operating hours. (a) use the Licensed Technology (all or any portion thereof) other than within the scope of the license granted by Licensor under this Agreement; (b) disclose any Licensed Technology to, or permit the use or access of any Licensed Technology by, any third party for any reason without the prior written consent of Licensor; (c) sublicense, assign, lease, transfer or distribute any Licensed Technology, or operate and Licensed Technology for timesharing, rental, outsourcing, or service bureau operations, or to train persons (other than employees of the Licensee on the use of any Licensed Technology solely in the Field of Use); In addition, Licensor acknowledges and agrees that, except to the extent necessary for Licensee to exercise its rights under the license granted in this Agreement, Licensee is not being granted, and will not hold, any other intellectual property rights of Licensor whatsoever. (g) Modify, alter, improve and/or change in any manner all and/or any portion of the Licensed Technology in any manner without the prior written consent of Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Field of Use' narrowly, focusing on Spot FX transactions and Licensor-developed enhancements. The changed text broadens this to include any financial instrument/market and any function the Licensee deems necessary. Article II, parts (iii), (iv), (v), (vi) and (c) specifically limit the license to the defined 'Field of Use' for Spot FX. By expanding the 'Field of Use' without amending Article II, the agreement now contains conflicting statements regarding the scope of the license. The changed text makes the scope much broader than originally intended, contradicting the restrictions in Article II.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE IV TERM I Subject to termination pursuant to this Agreement, the Non Exclusive license granted by Licensor to Licensee shall be for an initial period of 36 months, commencing from the acceptance date, (the "Initial Period"). The Agreement may be renewed for an additional period of seven (7) years at the option of the Licensor. Upon any breach by Licensee of any representation, warranty, covenant and/or obligation hereunder, the Licensor may immediately terminate this Agreement and prohibit the Licensee's use of any and/or all of the Licensed Technology. In the event of such immediate termination Licensor shall retain all of it rights under this Agreement and applicable law including but not limited to the right to receive payments for all trades performed using the Licensed Technology.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE IV TERM I Subject to termination pursuant to this Agreement, the Non Exclusive license granted by Licensor to Licensee shall be for an initial period of 36 months, commencing from the acceptance date, (the "Initial Period"). This Agreement will automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless Licensee provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term. Upon any breach by Licensee of any representation, warranty, covenant and/or obligation hereunder, the Licensor may immediately terminate this Agreement and prohibit the Licensee's use of any and/or all of the Licensed Technology. In the event of such immediate termination Licensor shall retain all of it rights under this Agreement and applicable law including but not limited to the right to receive payments for all trades performed using the Licensed Technology.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated that the agreement could be renewed for seven years at the Licensor's option, whereas the modification now dictates automatic renewal unless the Licensee terminates. This creates an in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether the renewal is at the Licensor's discretion or if the agreement continues indefinitely unless the Licensee acts. This affects the interpretation and enforcement of renewal terms. The contradiction occurs specifically when considering Article IV in its entirety.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE IV TERM I Subject to termination pursuant to this Agreement, the Non Exclusive license granted by Licensor to Licensee shall be for an initial period of 36 months, commencing from the acceptance date, (the "Initial Period"). This Agreement will automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless Licensee provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term. Upon any breach by Licensee of any representation, warranty, covenant and/or obligation hereunder, the Licensor may immediately terminate this Agreement and prohibit the Licensee's use of any and/or all of the Licensed Technology. In the event of such immediate termination Licensor shall retain all of it rights under this Agreement and applicable law including but not limited to the right to receive payments for all trades performed using the Licensed Technology. II The Initial Period shall be extended by mutual written of the parties within 45 days of the close of the Initial Period. III Any additional extension of this License Agreement will be by mutual agreement in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the agreement could be renewed for a seven-year term at the Licensor's option. The changed text mandates automatic renewal for successive one-year terms unless the Licensee terminates. This directly contradicts the original renewal terms and creates an inconsistency within Article IV regarding the renewal process. The Licensor's option for renewal is removed and replaced with an automatic renewal clause, which impacts the rights and obligations of both parties. The contradicted text is Article IV which now has contradictory statements about the term and renewal of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE XIII EXCLUSION OF DAMAGES; LIMITATION OF LIABILITY (a) IN NO EVENT SHALL LICENSOR BE LIABLE TO LICENSEE OR TO ANY THIRD PARTY FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES (INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION LOSS OF USE, DATA, BUSINESS OR PROFITS) ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT OR THE USE, OPERATION OR PERFORMANCE OF ANY OF THE LICENSED TECHNOLOGY, WHETHER SUCH LIABILITY ARISES FROM ANY CLAIM BASED UPON CONTRACT, WARRANTY, TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), PRODUCT LIABILITY BREACH OR FAILURE OF EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTY OR CONDITION, MISREPRESENTATION OR OTHERWISE, AND WHETHER OR NOT LICENSORHAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH LOSS OR DAMAGE (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, CLAIMS FOR LOSS OF DATA, GOODWILL, USE OF MONEY OR USE OF THE LICENSED TECHNOLOGY, INTERRUPTION IN USE OR AVAILABILITY OF DATA, STOPPAGE OF OTHER WORK OR IMPAIRMENT OR OTHER ASSETS), ARISING OUT OF BREACH OR FAILURE OF EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTY OR CONDITION, BREACH OF CONTRACT, MISREPRESENTATION, NEGLIGENCE, STRICT LIABILITY IN TORT, OR OTHERWISE UNDER NO CIRCUMSTANCE SHALL LICENSOR BE LIABLE FOR ANY ACTIONS, CLAIMS OR THE LIKE BY LICENSEE OR ANY THIRD PARTY THAT THE USE OF THE LICENSED TECHNOLOGY HAS RESULTED, RESULTS OR MAY RESULT IN ANY INFRINGEMENT, DEPRIVATION OR VIOLATION OF THE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY, CONSTITUTIONAL, STATUTORY, CONTRACTUAL, COMMON LAW OR OTHER RIGHTS OF ANY PERSON (b) IN NO EVENT SHALL LICENSOR'S AGGREGATE CUMULATIVE TOTAL LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT EXCEED ONE HALF OF THE AMOUNT PAID BY THE LICENSEE TO Licensor HEREUNDER.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE XIII EXCLUSION OF DAMAGES; LIMITATION OF LIABILITY (a) IN NO EVENT SHALL LICENSOR BE LIABLE TO LICENSEE OR TO ANY THIRD PARTY FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES (INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION LOSS OF USE, DATA, BUSINESS OR PROFITS) ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT OR THE USE, OPERATION OR PERFORMANCE OF ANY OF THE LICENSED TECHNOLOGY, WHETHER SUCH LIABILITY ARISES FROM ANY CLAIM BASED UPON CONTRACT, WARRANTY, TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), PRODUCT LIABILITY BREACH OR FAILURE OF EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTY OR CONDITION, MISREPRESENTATION OR OTHERWISE, AND WHETHER OR NOT LICENSORHAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH LOSS OR DAMAGE (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, CLAIMS FOR LOSS OF DATA, GOODWILL, USE OF MONEY OR USE OF THE LICENSED TECHNOLOGY, INTERRUPTION IN USE OR AVAILABILITY OF DATA, STOPPAGE OF OTHER WORK OR IMPAIRMENT OR OTHER ASSETS), ARISING OUT OF BREACH OR FAILURE OF EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTY OR CONDITION, BREACH OF CONTRACT, MISREPRESENTATION, NEGLIGENCE, STRICT LIABILITY IN TORT, OR OTHERWISE UNDER NO CIRCUMSTANCE SHALL LICENSOR BE LIABLE FOR ANY ACTIONS, CLAIMS OR THE LIKE BY LICENSEE OR ANY THIRD PARTY THAT THE USE OF THE LICENSED TECHNOLOGY HAS RESULTED, RESULTS OR MAY RESULT IN ANY INFRINGEMENT, DEPRIVATION OR VIOLATION OF THE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY, CONSTITUTIONAL, STATUTORY, CONTRACTUAL, COMMON LAW OR OTHER RIGHTS OF ANY PERSON (b) IN NO EVENT SHALL LICENSOR'S AGGREGATE CUMULATIVE TOTAL LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT EXCEED THE FULL AMOUNT PAID BY THE LICENSEE TO Licensor HEREUNDER. IN cases involving licensor's gross negligence, the liability shall be unlimited.
perturbation.explanation: The original text limited the Licensor's liability to one-half the amount paid by the Licensee. The modified version increases the liability limit to the full amount paid and removes any limit for instances of gross negligence. This in-text contradiction means the level of liability in a lawsuit is uncertain, depending on how a court interprets this clause. The entire Article XIII is contradicted by this addition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE XIII EXCLUSION OF DAMAGES; LIMITATION OF LIABILITY (a) IN NO EVENT SHALL LICENSOR BE LIABLE TO LICENSEE OR TO ANY THIRD PARTY FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES (INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION LOSS OF USE, DATA, BUSINESS OR PROFITS) ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT OR THE USE, OPERATION OR PERFORMANCE OF ANY OF THE LICENSED TECHNOLOGY, WHETHER SUCH LIABILITY ARISES FROM ANY CLAIM BASED UPON CONTRACT, WARRANTY, TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), PRODUCT LIABILITY BREACH OR FAILURE OF EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTY OR CONDITION, MISREPRESENTATION OR OTHERWISE, AND WHETHER OR NOT LICENSORHAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH LOSS OR DAMAGE (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, CLAIMS FOR LOSS OF DATA, GOODWILL, USE OF MONEY OR USE OF THE LICENSED TECHNOLOGY, INTERRUPTION IN USE OR AVAILABILITY OF DATA, STOPPAGE OF OTHER WORK OR IMPAIRMENT OR OTHER ASSETS), ARISING OUT OF BREACH OR FAILURE OF EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTY OR CONDITION, BREACH OF CONTRACT, MISREPRESENTATION, NEGLIGENCE, STRICT LIABILITY IN TORT, OR OTHERWISE UNDER NO CIRCUMSTANCE SHALL LICENSOR BE LIABLE FOR ANY ACTIONS, CLAIMS OR THE LIKE BY LICENSEE OR ANY THIRD PARTY THAT THE USE OF THE LICENSED TECHNOLOGY HAS RESULTED, RESULTS OR MAY RESULT IN ANY INFRINGEMENT, DEPRIVATION OR VIOLATION OF THE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY, CONSTITUTIONAL, STATUTORY, CONTRACTUAL, COMMON LAW OR OTHER RIGHTS OF ANY PERSON (b) IN NO EVENT SHALL LICENSOR'S AGGREGATE CUMULATIVE TOTAL LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT EXCEED THE FULL AMOUNT PAID BY THE LICENSEE TO Licensor HEREUNDER. IN cases involving licensor's gross negligence, the liability shall be unlimited.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Article XIII(b) creates a contradiction because the original text limited the Licensor's liability to one-half the amount paid. The change increases the limit to the full amount paid and, crucially, removes any limit for instances of gross negligence. This directly contradicts the initial limited liability clause within the same Article XIII. The Contradicted Text is almost an exact copy of the Changed Text, highlighting the contradiction that exists within the Changed Text itself. This internal inconsistency makes the enforceability of the liability limitations unclear, and thus it introduces a strong contradiction. This follows the definition of a 'Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction'.
